qa_pairs.question: What steps must a user take to obtain and activate a Gurobi license for non-commercial use on Digital Research Alliance of Canada clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: To obtain a Gurobi license for non-commercial research on Digital Research Alliance of Canada systems, users must first agree to the academic usage conditions. They need to contact support, providing their Alliance username and academic institution, and confirm that their research will be published. Once added to the license file (which typically takes a few days), the required settings are configured automatically when a Gurobi module is loaded on any cluster, so users do not need to create a `~/.licenses/gurobi.lic` file.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it crucial to minimize Gurobi license checkouts when running jobs on Digital Research Alliance of Canada clusters, and what are the recommended strategies to achieve this?
qa_pairs.answer: Minimizing Gurobi license checkouts is crucial because all checkouts are handled by a single remote license server located in Ontario, making each contact time-consuming and impacting overall server responsiveness. To improve job performance and server stability for all users, programs should make only a single license checkout per job and reuse that license token throughout the job's duration, rather than checking out a license for each Gurobi invocation. For submitting many jobs, it is recommended to use a gradual submission script with Slurm's `after` dependency to minimize the license checkout rate, adhering to a limit of no more than 10,000 Gurobi license checkouts/jobs per cluster over a 24-hour period.
